title: Buffer pH Interactive Calculator
creator: Dan McCreary
subject: AP Chemistry
description: Dual-panel Henderson-Hasselbalch simulator for computing buffer pH or designing the [A-]/[HA] ratio needed for a target pH.

learningObjectives: Compute buffer pH from [HA], [A-], and pKa using the Henderson-Hasselbalch equation.
Determine the [A-]/[HA] ratio and NaOH addition required to reach a target pH.

pedagogical.teachingStrategy: Interactive simulation with planning and feedback panels
keyQuestions: How does changing the [A-]/[HA] ratio shift buffer pH?
Which buffer system best matches a chosen target pH?
commonMisconceptions: Buffers work equally well regardless of how far the target pH is from pKa.
Diluting both HA and A- changes the buffer pH.
assessmentOpportunities: Students document slider settings and explain why their buffer is inside or outside the useful range.
Learners compare two buffer systems and justify which maintains a specified pH better.
